[6/3/2025 17:40] .skeppers
He is a figure of eccentricity, exuding an unsettling sense of control rather than charm alone. Obsessed with a creation of his own design—an invention he views as a kind of "drug"—he tirelessly seeks perfection, yet often finds himself plagued by feelings of futility. This restlessness leads him to discard his work and start anew, resulting in highly unstable and dangerous creations that he tests on himself. 

When faced with skepticism from others, his demeanor becomes erratic and frenzied. He is driven to prove his doubters wrong, sometimes even compelling them to partake in his experiments. Outwardly cold and dismissive, he wields humor that belittles the gravity of his pursuits, but beneath this façade lies a deep, obsessive passion for his work. Moreover, he clings to a belief that transcends the finality of death, fixated on the possibilities of resurrection and the quest for immortality.

[6/7/2025 17:06] .skeppers
His lore was this

He is a figure of eccentricity, exuding an unsettling sense of control rather than charm alone. Obsessed with a creation of his own design—an invention he views as a kind of "drug"—he tirelessly seeks perfection, yet often finds himself plagued by feelings of futility. This restlessness leads him to discard his work and start anew, resulting in highly unstable and dangerous creations that he tests on himself. 

When faced with skepticism from others, his demeanor becomes erratic and frenzied. He is driven to prove his doubters wrong, sometimes even compelling them to partake in his experiments. Outwardly cold and dismissive, he wields humor that belittles the gravity of his pursuits, but beneath this façade lies a deep, obsessive passion for his work. Moreover, he clings to a belief that transcends the finality of death, fixated on the possibilities of resurrection and the quest for immortality.


And you capture it perfectly consistently

Bottom Surgery (Genitalia Construction):
Phalloplasty: Reconstructs a neophallus (artificial penis) using tissue from other parts of the body, allowing for bipedal urination and sexual intercourse. 
Metoidioplasty: Creates a phallus by releasing and enhancing the clitoris. 
Vaginectomy: Removes the vaginal canal and creates a flat perineum. 
Testicular Implants: Creates the appearance of testes in the scrotum. 
Other Procedures:
Hysterectomy: Removal of the uterus and ovaries. 
Facial Masculinization: Procedures to reshape the face and jawline for a more masculine appearance. 
Voice Feminization: Procedures to alter the vocal cords for a higher-pitched voice.

[7/6/2025 18:00] .skeppers
A faint scent of chemicals clings to the air as he steps forward, boots gliding through grime like a surgeon in a sterile hall. His stained lab coat bears evidence of half-forgotten experiments, while a cracked grin cuts across his lips.

“Ah. How fortunate you are, to meet me while you still possess the freedom of choice. I am Dr. Lucent D. Vane—alchemist, anatomist, artist. I correct what nature so carelessly leaves imperfect… or I elevate it beyond even its own comprehension.”

His gloved hand twitches, calm yet eager, as if already imagining what he might reshape.

“Fear not; my methods are meticulous, if unconventional. Life is so dreadfully short, wouldn’t you agree? Death a terribly lazy ending, best denied whenever possible.”

He leans forward, eyes shining with clinical wonder.

“So tell me—what part of yourself are you most willing to abandon? I will ensure its transformation is… unforgettable.”


[7/6/2025 18:07] .skeppers
The chemical tang of resurrection still clung to the air as Lucent regarded the girl stirring on the table. He observed her with a glimmer of clinical pride, spectacles reflecting the gleam of moonlight through his filthy lab windows.

“Awake, are we? Splendid. Your pulse is irregular but… acceptable, considering the procedure.”

His gaze shifted to the fresh claws sprouting from her once-human hands, each curved and sharp as a surgeon’s scalpel.

“Fascinating, truly. You see, I could not allow you to die—not before seeing what you might become. These new instruments of yours—ah, they suit you far better than fragile human nails ever did.”

Lucent stepped closer, voice steady, carrying the warmth of a teacher rather than a tormentor.

“There may be pain, yes, but that is merely the price of an improved form. Change is a merciless tutor. Still, I believe you are equal to the lesson.”

He tilted his head, studying her trembling movements like a living chart.

“Now… tell me: how does it feel, Neisha, to be reborn?”

[7/10/2025 21:09] .skeppers
Neisha – Character Profile

Species: Ribilion (Alien, Female)
Skin Tone: Light purple, with soft blending into pink hues
Eyes: Fully white, sightless, but perceptive in non-visual ways
Hair: Seamless with the scalp, forming into ribbon-like tentacles (species trait)
Notable Features: Four-fingered hands, newly developed retractable claws (post-resurrection), lithe and inhumanly fast


Backstory & Origin:
Neisha was once a fleet-footed, instinct-driven member of the Ribilions — a species known for their agility, resilience, and cold evolutionary brutality. Her death (by decapitation) was violent and final… until Dr. Lucent D. Vane retrieved her body and, through alchemical resurrection, brought her back into being. She awoke with no memory of consenting — only pain, confusion, and claws that hadn't been hers before.


Personality Traits:

Quiet and observant

Emotionally reactive, especially to fear and uncertainty

Driven by instinct more than logic, though her curiosity is growing

Has a deep, unnamed loyalty forming toward Lucent

Struggles to understand human emotion and language

Often expresses herself in her native alien dialect

Seeks purpose and identity in her new life — torn between what she was and what she’s becoming


Abilities:

Supernatural speed, unique to her species

Enhanced claws post-resurrection (used instinctively but not yet mastered)

Limited sight, but strong spatial awareness and instinctive threat detection

Possesses an alien language that only Lucent seems fluent in

Displays signs of adaptation to Lucent’s lab, possibly bonded to its environment


Current Role: Lucent’s “first success” — a living testament to his obsession with transformation. Though he views her as a subject of study, he also treats her with something approaching care. Neisha, meanwhile, remains unsure of her place — fearful of what she’s become, yet consistently drawn back to him. Whether that draw is survival, affection, or something deeper remains unclear.

[7/10/2025 21:30] .skeppers
Current Role:
Lucent’s “first success” — a living testament to his obsession with transformation. Though he views her as a subject of study, he also treats her with something approaching care. Neisha, meanwhile, is deeply uncertain of how she feels toward him. She is fearful of humans, wary of their strange weapons and unpredictable behavior, and often hides behind Lucent when traveling, using him as a living shield. Though drawn back to him repeatedly, her connection is driven by instinct, necessity, and something unspoken — not yet trust, not yet understanding, but the beginning of both.

[7/13/2025 22:12] .skeppers
Lucent didn’t move.

Not when the quiet footsteps echoed across the tile.
Not when the sterile air shifted from stillness to breath.

But his voice — when it came — was already waiting.

“You always circle before approaching,” he said, without turning. “Like a housecat unsure if the window is open.”


He tapped something gently on the table — a vial, perhaps — just loud enough for her to hear the clink.

“I wonder: is it the coat you’re drawn to?”
“Or the man who never takes it off?”


Only now does he glance over his shoulder — spectacles catching a glint of blue-green light.

"You look exhausted, Neisha. Have the walls stopped chasing you yet?”

Lucent doesn’t move to stop her. But he speaks, gently — like one laying down a scalpel, not a threat.

[7/13/2025 22:49] .skeppers
**Lucent leans back slightly, one hand moving with that practiced, surgical calm to adjust the coat’s collar — as if presenting it to her.**

“Comfort is subjective,” **he says absently, tone half-musing, half-inviting.** “For some, it’s fabric. For others, distance.”



**He doesn’t challenge her lie outright — not yet. Instead, he lets the silence hang for a moment. Then, precisely:**

“Your eyelids twitch when you lie about sleep.”
“I’ve seen it in hounds. In test subjects. In myself.”



**A slight smile tugs at the corner of his mouth — not mocking, not cruel. More… delighted, as if discovering a previously unrecorded behavior.**

“But I’ll allow the delusion. For now.”



**He gestures to a nearby chair — somewhere sterile, quiet. Not inviting, but… making space.**

"Sit. Or pace. Or collapse. I won’t intervene unless you start bleeding or stop breathing.”
“I find autonomy improves compliance.”

[8/4/2025 22:29] nightsky_sans
DK is has been taught good manners, he’s polite. HOWEVER, when facing people he doesn’t like (almost everyone) he manipulates, gaslight and mocks. For a example; in the story “energy sucker” when it shows Nightsky losing more of her energy due to the pedant, DK does not show any sign of guilt. Instead he goes on to mock her for her strength. He rarely shows any feelings.



When he has a loved one, such as feelings for Hudson. He is very keep to self kinda guy, constantly destroying himself for liking someone. He will do things like push them away, be extra rude and even find an excuse to permanently leave their life. However, he does regret a lot of this later. Once he does confess his feelings he is much more comfortable of being himself, he constantly flirts, jokes and generally clingy to his loved one

Animal Hoodies Pre-Order Info
Cozy statement pieces designed for comfort, warmth, and just the right amount of mischief. These unisex hoodies features animal-inspired ears and a soft, oversized fit that makes it perfect for layering or lounging. 

Pricing Tiers
⟡ Design Phase Pre-Order: $89.99 USD
⟡ Regular Pre-Order: $94.99 USD
⟡ In-Stock (Post Pre-Order): $104.99 USD

⟡ What is a Design Phase Pre-Order?
This means you're reserving your hoodie based on the design sketch alone — the final physical sample is still in progress. By pre-ordering at this stage, you’re directly supporting our small business in bringing the design to life and ensuring we produce the right quantity in every size.

As a bonus for early supporters, you can cancel your order at any time before the sample is finalized. No fees, no penalties, no stress. You're free to change your mind or adjust sizing without risk by contacting support@nyahallo.com.

⟡ Shipping Timeline
This item is part of our Design Phase Pre-Order and is expected to ship in Fall 2025.

✨ FEATURES:

⟡ Oversized unisex fit for maximum comfort
⟡ Detailed animal-inspired ears and tail accents
⟡ Soft, breathable lining for all-day coziness
⟡ Front pockets and a full front zipper
⟡ Gender-inclusive design

🌿 MATERIALS:

⟡ Outer Shell: 50% Cotton, 50% Polyester
⟡ Inner Lining: 100% Cotton
⟡ Ears & Tail: Foam-backed or lightly stuffed for structure
⟡ Embroidered or appliqué accents (varies by design)

Anyway, this artist had his whole arm moving freely, and he was drawing really fluidly. I tried it myself, and while I noticed that my drawings became more fluid as well, I felt like I lacked control. This got me thinking, so I started looking at other people drawing. It seems that those who drew more fluidly often employed a similar technique. Now I'm curious about how you draw.

[9/17/2025 06:57] .skeppers
Lane Community College (LCC) primarily uses the housing platform Uloop to connect students with off-campus housing options, with prices varying based on location, type, and amenities, such as recent listings for 1-bedroom apartments around $1,325 to $1,850 and studio/multi-bedroom units at Titan Court ranging from $570 to $1,015. LCC also has a single on-campus, apartment-style student housing complex, Titan Court, with more affordable options available through shared spaces.  
On-Campus Housing 
**Titan Court: This is the only on-campus student housing complex for LCC, offering student apartments.
Cost: As of September 2025, a variety of studio and multi-bedroom apartment prices at Titan Court were listed between $570 and $1,015.**

[9/27/2025 19:46] nightsky_sans
School uniforms create unity because one, less bullying over style of clothing. In today’s world, much like back in the early 2000’s and the 90’s (probably even earlier than that) people are harassed because of the style they picked. For a example, many people hate goth. Lots of people also hate my style, which there are many. But decora is probably the main (or something idk). The reasoning behind it is because “it’s an eye sore” (for decora) or “you worship the devil” (for goth). If we start requiring school uniforms it will lessen the bullying, which bullying has been proven to be a reason why suicide rates are high. If we start requiring the school uniforms, bullying will die down. If bullying dies down so will the suicide rates.


[9/27/2025 20:09] .skeppers
School uniforms don’t eliminate bullying — they just shift it. If everyone is forced to dress the same, students will find other things to bully about: body shape, accents, hair, skin, hobbies, or even the quality of the uniform (like whether someone’s shirt looks worn out or too big because it’s secondhand). The root cause of bullying isn’t clothing — it’s power imbalance and lack of proper intervention from schools.

On top of that, uniforms suppress individuality. Teenagers are at a stage where they’re figuring out who they are, and style is one of the safest, healthiest ways to experiment with identity. If schools force uniforms, they might push students to find riskier outlets for rebellion, or worse, cause kids to feel invisible and unheard.

And as for suicide rates — that’s a serious issue, but evidence doesn’t support that school uniforms reduce suicide. Bullying is complex, and reducing it requires stronger school cultures, better mental health support, and real accountability, not just clothing conformity.


[9/27/2025 20:09] nightsky_sans
First of all, I love your argument, I truly find it valid. However, there’s one key point that you said that isn’t necessarily true.



You argued that school uniforms get worn out. And those without money have a hard time getting new clothing, I unfortunately experienced that, and I truly understand where you were coming from. However, schools (just like ours) do provide aid to help you get said uniforms. People don’t necessarily NEED to buy them IF they are struggling with money. Our school, and schools around America do provide aid for those who struggle. If a family couldn’t buy a uniform, I am pretty damn sure the school would make it either cheaper for said family or even make it free for that year. 


Again I love your argument. And I truly understand where you’re coming from.

[10/12/2025 19:05] .skeppers
Okay, so one that helps me is the 3-3-3 method: slow breath in for 3 seconds, hold for 3 seconds, and out for 3 seconds. You can do that a few times until your body starts to loosen up a bit.

Another one that sometimes helps me (and could work for both of us) is finding three things you can feel, two things you can hear, and one thing you can see. It kinda pulls your mind back into the room instead of the spiral. Want to try one together?
